plan_adherence_notes: All 8 plan steps were executed with corresponding files created. Command pattern (step 1), HistoryContext with 50-cap (step 2), assumption command wiring (step 3), draft buffer (step 4), MarketDetail provider restructuring (step 5), toolbar UI (step 6), keyboard shortcuts with input filtering (step 7), and tests (step 8) all match the plan. However, BayesNetGraph.test.tsx (13 pre-existing tests) broke because BayesNetGraph now calls useAssumptions but the test wrapper was not updated with AssumptionProvider. The implementation.json claims all 63 new tests pass but does not acknowledge the 13 regressions in BayesNetGraph.test.tsx. This is a meaningful gap — the execution did not fully verify existing tests still pass after the changes.
missed_questions: Should useHistoryAssumptions replace useAssumptions in existing consumers (VariableRow, AssumptionPanel) or be opt-in? The current implementation creates the hook but it's unclear if existing components were migrated to use it.
How should accessibility be handled for the undo/redo toolbar (aria-labels exist but no screen reader announcement of undo/redo actions)?
What testing strategy should be used — should existing test suites be updated for the new provider requirements (BayesNetGraph now requires AssumptionProvider)?
reason: Implementation is solid and follows the plan well, but 13 pre-existing BayesNetGraph tests are broken (useAssumptions must be used within AssumptionProvider). BayesNetGraph was modified to call useAssumptions but BayesNetGraph.test.tsx was not updated to wrap the component in AssumptionProvider. This regression must be fixed before approval. The new undo/redo feature tests (HistoryContext, commands, useDraftEdits, useUndoRedoKeyboard) all pass correctly.
